mechanism_narrative: Faster PAYEMS growth raises expected household income and aggregate earnings, so an always-on positive payroll-growth trend predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 1.00% after a low-rate period signals tighter discount-rate conditions, reducing the present value of equity cash flows and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS upward crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises over 3 months while CPIAUCSL inflation does not accelerate, real consumption momentum improves expected revenues without margin pressure, predicting positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS level was 363696.0 and CPIAUCSL level was 218.813
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction coefficient is not positive and significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward logistic forecasts of 20-day return direction.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO together with a falling UNRATE signals rising capacity utilization supported by labor demand, lifting earnings expectations and predicting positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO level was 107.254 and UNRATE level was 6.5
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 1 percentage point on average out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST crossing below 900 thousand units signals weakening housing demand and reduced construction-linked activity, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and predicting negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below-900 triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward events.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.50% erodes real policy accommodation credibility and raises future tightening risk, predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation crosses above 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < 1.50%
regime_hint: inflation scare under low nominal policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if this inflation-policy interaction has a positive average 20-day forward return or a nonnegative classification coefficient in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows and RSAFS contracts, broad demand weakness reduces expected corporate revenue growth, so the joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth crosses below 0 or RSAFS 3-month change crosses below -1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered demand-deterioration cases do not produce lower mean 60-day returns than non-triggered cases at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE crossing upward by more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months signals deteriorating labor conditions and recession-risk repricing, predicting negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE upward-break triggers have a hit rate below 55% for negative 5-day S&P 500 returns across walk-forward samples.
